output.code:
import re

text = open('/mnt/data/file-FHPts9WEWkVWKwXkaig3M7-text_to_analyse.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()

citations = []

# Helper to add citation
def add_citation(start, end, label):
    citations.append({'start':start, 'end':end, 'text':text[start:end], 'label':label})

# 1) initial Quran quote in double quotes
dq_matches = list(re.finditer(r'\"([^\"]+)\"', text))
for m in dq_matches:
    # We will classify later based on content
    add_citation(m.start(1), m.end(1), 'double_quoted')

# 2) Hadith: "إن في الجنة مائة درجة..." first occurrence after "كما ذكر الحديث الشريف ... قال: "
pattern1 = r'قال:\s*إن في الجنة مائة درجة[^.\n]+'
m1 = re.search(pattern1, text)
if m1:
    add_citation(m1.start(), m1.end(), 'hadith_100_degrees_main')

# 3) Paraphrase: "أرواح الشهداء في أجواف طير خضر معلقة تحت العرش،"
m2 = re.search(r'أرواح الشهداء[^،.]+', text)
if m2:
    add_citation(m2.start(), m2.end(), 'hadith_birds_paraphrase')

# 4) "يغفر لهم كل ذنب..." snippet in same sentence
m2b = re.search(r'يغفر لهم كل ذنب[^.،]+', text)
if m2b:
    add_citation(m2b.start(), m2b.end(), 'hadith_maghfirah_paraphrase')

# 5) "للشهيد عند الله ست خصال: ... (until period)"
m3 = re.search(r'للشهيد عند الله ست خصال:\s*[^.]+', text)
if m3:
    add_citation(m3.start(), m3.end(), 'hadith_6_merits_main')

# 6) "قال: من سأل الله الشهادة ..."
m4 = re.search(r'قال:\s*من سأل الله الشهادة[^.]+', text)
if m4:
    add_citation(m4.start(), m4.end(), 'hadith_ask_martyrdom_main')

# 7) In references [1]: ((...)) hadith triple "من قتل دون دمه ..."
for m in re.finditer(r'⟦', text):
    pass
# find occurrences inside double parentheses (( ... ))
parenparen = list(re.finditer(r'⟦', text))
# Actually the text uses double parentheses (()). We'll detect "(( ... ))"
pp = list(re.finditer(r'\(\(([^\)]+)\)\)', text))
for m in pp:
    add_citation(m.start(1), m.end(1), 'double_parentheses')


# 8) Ayah in reference [1] inside parentheses just before (آل عمران
m5 = re.search(r'كما قال تعالى:\s*\(([^)]+)\)\s*\(آل عمران', text)
if m5:
    # This contains concatenated 169-171; we'll split inside, but also store the whole span for debugging
    paren_start = m5.start(1); paren_end = m5.end(1)
    paren_text = text[paren_start:paren_end]
    # identify 3 segments by keywords
    # seg1 from start to 'يُرْزَقُونَ'
    e1 = paren_text.find('يُرْزَقُونَ')
    if e1 != -1:
        seg1_start = 0
        seg1_end = e1 + len('يُرْزَقُونَ')
        add_citation(paren_start+seg1_start, paren_start+seg1_end, 'quran_3_169_ref1')
    # seg2 from after e1 to find end 'يَحْزَنُونَ'
    s2 = seg1_end if e1 != -1 else 0
    e2 = paren_text.find('يَحْزَنُونَ', s2)
    if e2 != -1:
        seg2_start = s2
        seg2_end = e2 + len('يَحْزَنُونَ')
        add_citation(paren_start+seg2_start, paren_start+seg2_end, 'quran_3_170_ref1')
    # seg3 from after e2 to end (assuming last)
    s3 = seg2_end if e2 != -1 else (e1+len('يُرْزَقُونَ') if e1!=-1 else 0)
    # 171 ends with 'الْمُؤْمِنِينَ' or depends on text 'الْمُؤْمِنِينَ)'
    e3 = paren_text.rfind('الْمُؤْمِنِينَ')
    if e3 != -1 and e3 >= s3:
        seg3_start = s3
        seg3_end = e3 + len('الْمُؤْمِنِينَ')
        add_citation(paren_start+seg3_start, paren_start+seg3_end, 'quran_3_171_ref1')

# 9) In [2]: Ayah in parentheses after "قوله: ("
m6 = re.search(r'قوله:\s*\(([^)]+)\)\s*\(٢\)', text)
if m6:
    add_citation(m6.start(1), m6.end(1), 'quran_3_169_ref2')

# 10) In [3]: hadith again "قال: إن في الجنة مائة درجة ..."
for m in re.finditer(r'صحيحه أن رسول الله .*? قال:\s*إن في الجنة مائة درجة[^.]+', text):
    add_citation(m.start(), m.end(), 'hadith_100_degrees_ref3')

# 11) In [3]: "قال: لما أصيب إخوانكم بأحد ..."
m7 = re.search(r'قال:\s*لما أصيب إخوانكم بأحد[^.]+', text)
if m7:
    add_citation(m7.start(), m7.end(), 'hadith_birds_ref3')

# 12) Verses in [3] after "وأنزل الله تعالى: "
m8 = re.search(r'وأنزل الله تعالى:\s*([^\.]+)\.', text)
if m8:
    seg = text[m8.start(1):m8.end(1)]
    # split by asterisks *
    parts = [p.strip() for p in seg.split('*')]
    offs = []
    acc = m8.start(1)
    # We'll find each part index using find to ensure exact positions
    for p in parts:
        if not p:
            continue
        idx = text.find(p, acc, m8.end(1))
        if idx != -1:
            add_citation(idx, idx+len(p), 'quran_3_169_171_ref3_part')
            acc = idx + len(p)

# 13) In [3]: "وقال ﷺ: للشهيد عند الله ست خصال: ..."
m9 = re.search(r'وقال ﷺ:\s*للشهيد عند الله ست خصال:[^.]+', text)
if m9:
    add_citation(m9.start(), m9.end(), 'hadith_6_merits_ref3')

# 14) In [4]: The full verse "وَالَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا ..."
m10 = re.search(r'قوله تعالى:\s*([وف]\S.+?)\s*\{الحج:58\}', text)
if m10:
    add_citation(m10.start(1), m10.end(1), 'quran_22_58_ref4')

# 15) The verse "ومن يخرج من بيته مهاجرًا ..."
m11 = re.search(r'قوله تعالى:\s*ومن يخرج من بيته مهاجرًا[^،]+', text)
if m11:
    add_citation(m11.start(), m11.end(), 'quran_4_100_ref4_raw')

# 16) The small hadith "أنت من الأولين"
m12 = re.search(r'أنت من الأولين', text)
if m12:
    add_citation(m12.start(), m12.end(), 'hadith_umm_haram_phrase')

# Review collected spans
len(citations), citations[:10]

# Remove the problematic 'quran_22_58_ref4' and 'quran_4_100_ref4_raw' entries
citations = [c for c in citations if c['label'] not in ('quran_22_58_ref4','quran_4_100_ref4_raw')]

# Re-detect verse 22:58 capturing specifically the verse text that precedes {الحج:58}
# Find the position of "{الحج:58}"
brace_pos = text.find('{الحج:58}')
start_pos = None
if brace_pos != -1:
    # Walk backward to find a likely start of the verse: search for 'قوله تعالى:' before that
    prefix_pos = text.rfind('قوله تعالى:', 0, brace_pos)
    if prefix_pos != -1:
        # verse text is between prefix_pos + len('قوله تعالى: ') and brace_pos - 1 
        verse_start = prefix_pos + len('قوله تعالى:')
        # Skip possible whitespace
        while verse_start < brace_pos and text[verse_start].isspace():
            verse_start += 1
        # From verse_start to just before the space before '{الحج:58}' 
        verse_text = text[verse_start:brace_pos].rstrip()
        # Remove trailing punctuation
        verse_text_stripped = verse_text.rstrip(' .،؛')
        verse_end = verse_start + len(verse_text_stripped)
        add_citation(verse_start, verse_end, 'quran_22_58_ref4_clean')

# For 4:100 "ومن يخرج من بيته مهاجرا"
# Find "ومن يخرج من بيته مهاجرًا" and capture until "،"
m = re.search(r'ومن يخرج من بيته مهاجرًا[^،]+', text)
if m:
    add_citation(m.start(), m.end(), 'quran_4_100_ref4_clean')

len(citations), [c for c in citations if 'ref4' in c['label']]

# Print all citations with index and text
for idx, c in enumerate(citations):
    print(idx, c['label'], c['start'], c['end'])
    print(c['text'])
    print('-'*80)

import os, glob, json, textwrap, sys, unicodedata, pathlib, itertools
glob.glob('/mnt/data/*')[:20]

# Clean up leading/trailing non-Arabic characters for some ayah spans
def trim_span(start, end):
    # Trim leading spaces and parentheses
    s = start
    e = end
    # Trim leading whitespace and punctuation like '(' ' ' '،'
    while s < e and text[s] in [' ', '(', '\n', '\t', '،']:
        s += 1
    # Trim trailing whitespace and punctuation like ')'
    while e > s and text[e-1] in [' ', ')', '\n', '\t', '،']:
        e -= 1
    return s, e

# Apply to labels containing 'quran_3_171_ref1' and 'quran_3_170_ref1' etc.
for c in citations:
    if c['label'].startswith('quran_'):
        new_s, new_e = trim_span(c['start'], c['end'])
        c['start'], c['end'] = new_s, new_e
        c['text'] = text[new_s:new_e]

# Reprint the specific ones with spaces
for idx in [11,12]:
    c = citations[idx]
    print(idx, c['label'], c['start'], c['end'])
    print(repr(c['text']))

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
